mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and compresses corporate profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 3 percent and FEDFUNDS rises, with positive forward returns occurring in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate revenue prospects, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns during economic expansions.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction during expansions, yielding positive forward returns in >60% of declining INDPRO periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with positive PAYEMS growth, or the signal shows no statistical significance with p-values above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.2 million units annually, construction spending and consumer confidence surge, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in less than 45% of periods when HOUST crosses the 1.2 million threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates robust capacity utilization and strong labor demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls, with negative forward returns dominating in >50% of cases during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining consumer price inflation reduces real interest rates and improves corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >60% of disinflationary periods, or the mechanism shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates.
